data: Respondents were asked how often in the past 30 days they felt that everything was an effort. Respondents could choose from among five response categories: "All of the time," "Most of the time," "Some of the time," "A little of the time," or "None of the time." "All" and "Most" are combined.

Respondents were asked how often in the past 30 days they felt that everything was an effort. Respondents could choose from among five response categories: "All of the time," "Most of the time," "Some of the time," "A little of the time," or "None of the time." "All" and "Most" are combined.
caption: Crude percentages of feeling that everything is an effort all or most of the time for adults aged 18-64 (standard errors), United States
caption3: Respondents were asked how often in the past 30 days they felt that everything was an effort. Respondents could choose from among five response categories: "All of the time," "Most of the time," "Some of the time," "A little of the time," or "None of the time." "All" and "Most" are combined.
